description: §6.13 throttle LEADING edge: the first write to a <val throttled=1s> cell in a quiescent window emits IMMEDIATELY (leading fire), synchronously — no timer is armed. One click drives @val 0 -> 42 and the #v interpolation + snapshot both read 42, with NO advance-time verb. Adversarial proof that the settled()-drain path works under the virtual clock: a synchronous leading-edge write drains via settle alone.
rationale: §6.13.2 throttle: a leading+trailing throttle emits the first write in any quiescent window immediately. Under the deterministic virtual clock (fixed non-zero epoch base), Date.now() at the first write is far past the zero-initialized lastEmit, so the elapsed-window test opens and the leading fire commits synchronously through the bypass window — no setTimeout is armed, so the case needs no advance-time and drains entirely via settled().
